settings.enableVanillaQuickRoll.name: Use Vanilla Rolls with RSReforged Styling
settings.enableVanillaQuickRoll.hint: Master switch for the quick-roll behavior. When enabled, RSReforged keeps dnd5e's normal roll dialogs and vanilla workflow (including automatic attack and damage rolls for item activities) and only adds RSReforged styling on top — the four 'Enable Quick Roll' options below are ignored. When disabled, RSReforged takes over enabled quick rolls and rolls attack, damage, healing, and formulas on the quick-roll card.
settings.enableAbilityQuickRoll.name: Enable Quick Roll for Abilities
settings.enableAbilityQuickRoll.hint: When clicking on an ability check or saving throw, quick roll to chat instead of using the normal roll dialog. Hold the dnd5e 'Skip Dialog' modifier (Shift by default) while clicking to use the normal dnd5e dialog for that roll. Has no effect when 'Use Vanilla Rolls with RSReforged Styling' is enabled.
settings.enableSkillQuickRoll.name: Enable Quick Roll for Skills
settings.enableSkillQuickRoll.hint: When clicking on a skill check, automatically roll to chat instead of using the normal roll dialog. Hold the dnd5e 'Skip Dialog' modifier (Shift by default) while clicking to use the normal dnd5e dialog for that roll. Has no effect when 'Use Vanilla Rolls with RSReforged Styling' is enabled.
settings.enableToolQuickRoll.name: Enable Quick Roll for Tool Checks
settings.enableToolQuickRoll.hint: When clicking on a tool check, quick roll to chat instead of using the normal roll dialog. Hold the dnd5e 'Skip Dialog' modifier (Shift by default) while clicking to use the normal dnd5e dialog for that roll. Has no effect when 'Use Vanilla Rolls with RSReforged Styling' is enabled.
settings.enableActivityQuickRoll.name: Enable Quick Roll for Activities
settings.enableActivityQuickRoll.hint: When clicking on an activity for an item, automatically roll to chat instead of using the normal roll dialog. Hold the dnd5e 'Skip Dialog' modifier (Shift by default) while clicking to use the normal dnd5e dialog for that activity. Has no effect when 'Use Vanilla Rolls with RSReforged Styling' is enabled.
settings.alwaysRollMulti.name: Always Roll Multiple Dice
settings.alwaysRollMulti.hint: When outputting a quick roll, always roll two dice (or three for Elven Accuracy) even when advantage/disadvantage is not applied.
settings.manualDamageMode.name: Roll Damage Manually
settings.manualDamageMode.hint: When outputting a quick roll, do not quick roll damage. Instead, show a damage button to manually roll if there is damage available.
settings.aggregateDamage.name: Aggregate Damage Fields
settings.aggregateDamage.hint: When outputting a quick roll, combine all damage fields of the same type into a single field.
settings.enableD20Icons.name: Show D20 Rolls for Quick Rolls
settings.enableD20Icons.hint: When outputting a quick roll, display an icon showing the natural die roll next to the total result.
settings.enableHideFinalResult.name: Hide Result of NPC Attacks
settings.enableHideFinalResult.hint: When outputting a quick roll with an attack made by an actor that a player does not own, hide the final modified result of the roll so that only the result of the d20 is visible.
settings.enableDiceReroll.name: Enable Dice Rerolling
settings.enableDiceReroll.hint: When outputting a quick roll, enable the ability to click a specific die in the chat message tooltip to reroll that die in place.
settings.enableOverlayButtons.name: Enable Overlay Buttons
settings.enableOverlayButtons.hint: When outputting a quick roll, enable overlay buttons on the chat message that offer additional functionality for the roll.
settings.damageApplyMode.name: Damage Apply UI
settings.damageApplyMode.hint: Choose one damage application interface for quick-roll damage cards. The dnd5e tray shows per-target rows and applies damage using the system's native workflow. RSReforged quick buttons show full/half/quarter/double controls directly on the damage roll.
settings.enableDamageButtons.name: Enable Damage Apply Buttons (Legacy)
settings.enableDamageButtons.hint: Legacy setting preserved for older worlds. Use Damage Apply UI instead.
settings.alwaysShowButtons.name: Always Show RSReforged Apply Buttons
settings.alwaysShowButtons.hint: Only applies when Damage Apply UI is set to RSReforged Quick Buttons. If disabled, those quick apply buttons appear only on hover.
settings.applyDamageTo.name: RSReforged Apply Button Targets
settings.applyDamageTo.hint: Only applies when Damage Apply UI is set to RSReforged Quick Buttons. Determines whether those buttons apply damage to selected tokens, targeted tokens, or both.
settings.confirmRetroAdv.name: Confirm Retroactive Advantage
settings.confirmRetroAdv.hint: Show a confirmation dialog when retroactively changing a roll to advantage or disadvantage.
settings.confirmRetroCrit.name: Confirm Retroactive Crits
settings.confirmRetroCrit.hint: Show a confirmation dialog when retroactively changing a roll to critical damage.
settings.rerollEveryone.name: Enable Interactive Dice (Master Switch)
settings.rerollEveryone.hint: Master switch for clickable dice rerolling and GM fudging. If disabled, no one can reroll dice by clicking, regardless of the per-role settings below.
settings.rerollPlayers.name: Allow Players to Reroll Their Own Dice
settings.rerollPlayers.hint: If enabled, players can left-click any die in the chat tooltip of a roll they made to reroll that die in place. GMs can always reroll any die.
settings.fudgeGM.name: Allow GM Dice Fudging
settings.fudgeGM.hint: If enabled, the GM can right-click any die in the chat tooltip to manually set its result via a prompt.
settings.rerollSoundEnabled.name: Reroll Sound & Dice So Nice
settings.rerollSoundEnabled.hint: When a die is rerolled, play the configured dice sound and animate the rerolled die in 3D via Dice So Nice if installed. If Dice So Nice is not available, the dice sound is still played as a fallback.
settings.rerollLogChat.name: Log Rerolls to Chat
settings.rerollLogChat.hint: When a die is rerolled, post a public chat message announcing the reroll (e.g. "Alice rerolled a d20: 7 → 14"). The message respects the current roll mode (public/whisper/blind/self).

buttons.damageFull: Click to apply full damage to selected token(s).
buttons.damageHalf: Click to apply half damage to selected token(s).
buttons.damageQuarter: Click to apply quarter damage to selected token(s).
buttons.damageDouble: Click to apply double damage to selected token(s).
buttons.healing: Click to apply healing to selected token(s).
buttons.healingTemp: Click to apply temporary hitpoints to selected token(s).
buttons.repeat: Click to repeat the quick roll.

error.incorrectTargetState: Cannot ugprade a roll to target state '{state}' because it is not a valid state (advantage or disadvantage).
error.rollIsNullOrUndefined: The given roll is null or undefined.
error.termIsNullOrUndefined: The given term is null or undefined.
error.applyDamageNoPermission: You do not have permission to apply damage from this message.
error.applyDamageNoTokens: You have no tokens selected or targeted to apply damage to.

warning.emptyDamageField: One or more damage fields on this item are empty! To roll damage, ensure all damage fields are filled.
